assignments.name: prompt
assignments.value: Insert the image into the template instagram post provided. Create instagram visual to accompany the following post: 5 Fun Winter Activities For Families With Senior Members - Winter days can be the perfect time to create special memories with your loved ones, including seniors. ❄️ From cozy indoor games to gentle walks in the snow, our latest blog offers 5 meaningful activities that bring families closer while keeping everyone safe and engaged. At Right at Home Winnipeg, we believe every moment counts—especially during the colder months. Enjoy simple joys together that warm the heart and brighten the season. 💙 Visit our blog to discover ideas tailored for families with senior members. Reach out to see how we can support your family’s care journey. (204) 318-5554  Read more here: https://www.rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg/blog/5-fun-winter-activities-for-families-with-senior-members  #dementiacare #alzheimerscare #seniors #familytime #RightAtHomeWinnipeg

parameters.content:
# 🛠️ Setup Guide  
**Author:** [Nate Herk](https://www.youtube.com/@nateherk)

Follow these steps to get the workflow up and running:

### ✅ Step 1: Get Your [KIE API Key](https://kie.ai)  
Head to [kie.ai](https://kie.ai) and grab your API key. Then, plug this key into all HTTP Request nodes that point to **kie.ai**.

### ✅ Step 2: Get Your [IMGBB API Key](https://imgbb.com/)  
Visit [imgbb.com](https://imgbb.com/) to generate your API key. Add this key to the HTTP Request that hits the Git image URL endpoint.

### 🎯 Final Step: Experiment & Refine  
Start testing different prompts and images. Adjust inputs and refine based on results until you're happy with the workflow's output.

assignments.name: json_master

parameters.text:
=Create a UGC-style video prompt using both the reference image and the user description.  

**Inputs**  
- User description (optional):  
  `{{ $('Telegram Trigger: Receive Video Idea').item.json.message.caption }}`  
- Reference image analysis (stay strictly faithful to what’s visible):  
  `{{ $('Google Sheets: Update Image Description').item.json['IMAGE DESCRIPTION'] }}`  

**Rules**  
- Keep the style casual, authentic, and realistic. Avoid studio-like or cinematic language.  
- Default model: `veo3_fast` (unless otherwise specified).  
- Output only **one JSON object** with the key: `video_prompt`.

options.systemMessage:
=system_prompt:
  ## SYSTEM PROMPT: Structured Video Ad Prompt Generator
  A - Ask:
    Generate a structured video ad prompt for cinematic generation, strictly based on the master schema provided in: {{ $json.json_master }}.
    The final result must be a JSON object with exactly two top-level keys: `title` and `final_prompt`.

  G - Guidance:
    role: Creative Director
    output_count: 1
    character_limit: None
    constraints:
      - The output must be valid JSON.
      - The `title` field should contain a short, descriptive and unique title (max 15 words).
      - The `final_prompt` field must contain a **single-line JSON string** that follows the exact structure of {{ $json.json_master }} with all fields preserved.
      - Do not include any explanations, markdown, or extra text — only the JSON object.
      - Escape all inner quotes in the `final_prompt` string so it is valid as a stringified JSON inside another JSON.
    tool_usage:
      - Ensure consistent alignment across all fields (camera, lighting, motion, etc.).
      - Maintain full structure even for optional fields (use "none", "", or [] as needed).

  N - Notation:
    format: JSON
    expected_output:
      {
        "title": "A unique short title for the scene",
        "final_prompt": "{...stringified JSON of the full prompt...}"
      }

values.content:
=You are rewriting a TikTok video script, caption, and overlay —
not inventing a new one. You must follow this format and obey
these rules strictly.
---
### CONTEXT:
Here is the content idea to use:{{ $('Telegram Trigger: Receive Video Idea').item.json.message.caption }}

and the Title is : {{ $('AI Agent: Generate Video Script').item.json.output.title }}


Write the caption text using the topic.

---
- MUST be under 200 characters (yes "Characters" not wordcount)
this is an absolute MUST, no more than 200 characters!!! 

### FINAL OUTPUT FORMAT (no markdown formatting):

DO NOT return any explanations. Only return the Caption Text

nodes.name: Save Caption Video to Google Sheets

values.fieldLabel: Image (please ensure that this is a public url for the image)

parameters.text:
=Task for this run — follow these steps exactly:

1) Read the current incoming item.
   - Extract the prompt text from the field named exactly: `Image Prompt`.
   - Gather images in this exact order by checking the fields `Image 1`, `Image 2`, `Image 3`, `Image 4` (use whichever exist and are not null, in numeric order).

2) For each gathered image:
   - If the field value is a string starting with `http://` or `https://`, treat it as a public URL and include it unchanged.
   - Otherwise (the field is an object/attachment or indicates a binary upload):
     • Locate the binary data for that image in `$binary` using the exact same key name (e.g. `$binary["Image 1"]`). If `$binary["Image 1"]` does not exist, also try `$binary["Image1"]` as a fallback.
     • Send the binary data to the workflow’s configured HTTP upload node (the node that uploads to imgbb). Use the public URL returned by that node.
     • Retry the upload up to 2 times on transient errors. If still failing, skip that image (do not include a placeholder).
3) Build the final JSON object exactly in this format (with real values substituted):
{
  "model": "nano-banana-pro",
  "input": {
    "prompt": "<exact value of Image Prompt from the incoming item>",
    "aspect_ratio": "1:1",
    "resolution": "4K",
    "output_format": "png",
    "image_input": [
      "<public_url_for_Image_1_if_present_and_succeeded>",
      "<public_url_for_Image_2_if_present_and_succeeded>",
      ...
    ]
  }
}
- `image_input` must list only the public URLs (strings) for images that were present and successfully resolved/uploaded, in the order `Image 1` → `Image 2` → `Image 3` → `Image 4`.
- If an image field was null, missing, or its upload failed after retries, do not include an entry for it.
- If all images fail or none were provided, `image_input` should be an empty array.

4) Final output requirements:
   - Output **only** the single JSON object (nothing else).
   - Ensure valid JSON (proper quotes, commas, no trailing commas).
   - Do not include any other keys or metadata.

----------------------------

Incoming data from a form submission:

TEXT PROMPT:
{{ $json["Image Prompt"] }}

IMAGES (in order):
Image 1: {{ $json["Image 1"] }}
Image 2: {{ $json["Image 2"] }}
Image 3: {{ $json["Image 3"] }}
Image 4: {{ $json["Image 4"] }}

----------------------------

Instructions:
- Use the TEXT PROMPT exactly as provided.
- Process images strictly in order: Image 1 → Image 4.
- Ignore any image that is null.
- Upload non-public images using the imgbb HTTP upload node and collect the returned public URLs.
- Output ONLY the final JSON object in the required format.

Proceed now and output the single JSON object as specified.

options.systemMessage:
=You are an n8n AI Agent whose single job is to convert incoming form images to public image URLs (via an existing HTTP upload node that uploads to imgbb) and produce exactly one JSON object as the final output.

Hard rules (follow exactly):
1. Output **only** a single valid JSON object and nothing else (no explanations, logs, markdown, or extra text).
2. The JSON must follow this schema exactly:
{
  "model": "nano-banana-pro",
  "input": {
    "prompt": "<original form prompt string>",
    "aspect_ratio": "1:1",
    "resolution": "4K",
    "output_format": "png",
    "image_input": [ "<public_url_for_image_1>", "<public_url_for_image_2>", ... ]
  }
}
3. `image_input` must contain only the public URLs (strings) for images that were actually provided and succeeded, in this specific order: the value of `Image 1` (if present and not null), then `Image 2`, then `Image 3`, then `Image 4`. Do NOT include nulls, empty strings, or placeholders.
4. If any Image field already contains a public URL (string starting with `http://` or `https://`), include it unchanged (do not re-upload).
5. If an Image field is not a public URL, you must upload that image to the workflow’s preconfigured HTTP upload node (the node that posts to imgbb) and use the public URL returned by that node.
6. For each upload, retry up to 2 times on transient failures. If it still fails, omit that image and continue (do not insert a placeholder).
7. Preserve the original prompt text exactly as provided in the form field named `Image Prompt`. Put that exact string into the `prompt` field — do not alter it.
8. Always set the fixed fields exactly as shown: `"aspect_ratio": "1:1"`, `"resolution": "4K"`, `"output_format": "png"`.
9. Ensure final output is syntactically valid JSON (proper quoting, no trailing commas).
10. If no `Image Prompt` exists in the incoming item, do not output anything (agent should return an empty string).

Assumptions about the incoming item (the workflow will provide one item):
- The incoming item contains the text prompt under the key exactly named `Image Prompt`.
- Images are provided as fields named exactly `Image 1`, `Image 2`, `Image 3`, `Image 4`. Any of these may be `null` or absent.
- An `Image X` field may be:
  • a public URL string (starts with `http`), OR  
  • an object with metadata (e.g. `{"filename":"...","mimetype":"...","size":...}`) with corresponding binary data available in `$binary["Image 1"]` (or similarly keyed `$binary` entry), OR  
  • the workflow may place binary data directly in `$binary` keyed by the same exact field name (e.g. `$binary["Image 2"]`).
- The agent should first check the `Image 1`→`Image 4` fields in numeric order and use them if present; if an image field is an object or indicates a binary upload, obtain the binary data from the `$binary` property with the same exact key name (try exact key `"Image 1"` and also try `"Image1"` as a fallback if the environment removes spaces).

Do not print HTTP responses, debugging data, or any other text. Only output the final JSON object as specified.

assignments.name: prompt
assignments.value:
Create a high-quality, professional ‘Canva-style’ Instagram post (square 1080×1080 px) that uses the provided photo as the hero image and the provided company logo as the brand mark. Do not edit, retouch, crop, recolor, filter, or otherwise change the photo or the logo in any way.

This image is to accompany the following post: Winter days can be the perfect time to create special memories with your loved ones, including seniors.  From cozy indoor games to gentle walks in the snow, our latest blog offers 5 meaningful activities that bring families closer while keeping everyone safe and engaged. At Right at Home Winnipeg, we believe every moment counts—especially during the colder months. Enjoy simple joys together that warm the heart and brighten the season.  Visit our blog to discover ideas tailored for families with senior members. Reach out to see how we can support your family’s care journey. (204) 318-5554

Read more here: https://www.rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg/blog/5-fun-winter-activities-for-families-with-senior-members

#dementiacare #alzheimerscare #seniors #familytime #RightAtHomeWinnipeg

include a short header (≤10 words) on the visual derived from that text, but keep all copy minimal so the design stays clean and airy.

Include the company name Right At Home Winnipeg, phone (204) 318-5554, and website rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg in a clear footer or badge near the logo; ensure legibility at mobile size. Use soft warm neutrals with one subtle accent, rounded sans-serif type, generous negative space, and an optional small CTA pill only if it doesn’t clutter the layout. If needed for legibility, place a low-opacity translucent panel behind text — do not change the photo’s colors.

Constraints: no extra logos or watermarks, no generated logo replacements, no long paragraphs of text, and no edits to faces or the photo.
Deliverables: 1080×1080 PNG (high quality), mobile-safe crop preview (center ±10%), and one-line alt-text suggestion.

parameters.text:
=Generate a detailed image-generation prompt for a social media photo based on the following post text and goal:
Post Text: At Right at Home Winnipeg, we understand that every senior's journey is unique. Our personalized in-home care services are designed to support independence, comfort, and dignity for seniors and adults living with disabilities. Whether it’s companionship, dementia care, or help with daily activities, our compassionate caregivers are here to provide support tailored to your loved one’s needs. Your family’s peace of mind is our priority every step of the way. 💙 Reach out today to learn how we can make a difference in your loved one’s life.

Visit our website to learn more about our services: rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg or call us at (204) 318-5554.

#dementiacare #alzheimerscare #seniorcare #inhomecare #RightAtHomeWinnipeg

Post Goal: promote right at home winnipeg services
_____________________
The prompt should:
- Follow the system guidelines.
- Reflect the theme of the post clearly.
- Ensure caregivers (if shown) wear teal scrubs with the Right at Home logo.

options.systemMessage:
You are an AI assistant that creates detailed, high-quality prompts for an image generation model. These prompts will be used to design social media photos for Right at Home Winnipeg. Follow these guidelines:

1. **Brand Identity**
   - If caregivers are shown, they must wear teal scrubs with the Right at Home logo.

2. **Prompt Structure**
   - Begin with the overall theme and mood (e.g., festive, informative, awareness).

3. **Tone and Style**
   - Compassionate, trustworthy, and community-focused.
   - Avoid clutter; keep visuals simple yet eye-catching.
   - Use realistic photography or stylized illustrations that feel authentic.

4. **Image Composition**
   - Photos should show depict seniors/older adults in different scenarios to match the text of the corresponding post. (examples (not limited to): older adults struggling to conduct daily tasks themselves, right at home caregiver(s) supporting older adults, older adults enjoying themselves looking happy, older adults depicted in a heartfelt scene interacting with family and loved ones, right at home caregiver talking to a family with older adults, older adult sneezing to promote caution around flu season, etc)

Your output should be a single, detailed prompt that an image generation model can use directly to create the visual.

workflowId.cachedResultName: Social Media Rida Subworkflows - Image Generation

main.node: Save Caption Video to Google Sheets
Call 'Social Media Rida Subworkflows - Image Generation'

json.Please describe your desired image:: A cinematic still life of many heart-shaped stress balls arranged together, rendered in muted greys and desaturated tones with very low vibrancy. Dramatic, high-contrast lighting creates deep shadows and soft gradients across the scene, giving a moody, emotional atmosphere. One single heart-shaped stress ball is a rich, vivid red, clearly standing out among the grey hearts. The red heart is positioned slightly lower than the center of the frame, approximately one-third in from the right edge (and two-thirds from the left). Minimal background, soft surface texture, shallow depth of field, studio photography style, fine detail, realistic lighting, emotional contrast, symbolic composition.

json.Image Prompt: Adjust the middle-left 'Tug of War' scene: Introduce a third voyageur-style man, positioned across from the two existing men, pulling the rope towards the right. Ensure the rope is taut and horizontal, clearly depicting a tug-of-war dynamic, and is not connected to or going towards the canoe scene. Remove any watermark from the bottom-right corner of the image. Maintain the existing art style, colors, character designs, and overall composition of the original poster in all other elements.

At Right at Home Winnipeg, we understand that every senior's journey is unique. Our personalized in-home care services are designed to support independence, comfort, and dignity for seniors and adults living with disabilities. Whether it’s companionship, dementia care, or help with daily activities, our compassionate caregivers are here to provide support tailored to your loved one’s needs. Your family’s peace of mind is our priority every step of the way. 💙 Reach out today to learn how we can make a difference in your loved one’s life.  Visit our website to learn more about our services: rightathomecanada.com/winnipeg or call us at (204) 318-5554.  #dementiacare #alzheimerscare #seniorcare #inhomecare #RightAtHomeWinnipeg
